# Yomi (読み)

> Yomi is an unofficial, open-source personal LINE MCP server that lets Claude Desktop,
> Claude Cowork through the desktop app, Claude Code, and other AI agents access
> the user's own LINE account. It reads and replies to messages, sends encrypted
> images, handles real mentions and read receipts, searches conversations
> through a local hybrid index, and surfaces which conversations are waiting on a
> reply as a compact relationship network.

Canonical product page: https://rikaidev.github.io/yomi/
Source: https://github.com/RikaiDev/yomi
Latest release: https://github.com/RikaiDev/yomi/releases/latest
License: MIT
Platforms: macOS Apple Silicon, Windows x64, Linux x64

## Installation

Claude Desktop users download the platform-specific `.mcpb` Desktop Extension
from the latest release, open it with Claude Desktop, review permissions, and
install. Claude Desktop supplies the runtime, so Node.js and Bun are not required.

Claude Code users run:

    claude mcp add yomi -- npx @rikaidev/yomi

The npm path requires Node.js 24 or newer. Bun is not required.

## Product facts

- Yomi targets personal LINE accounts and existing conversations. LINE's
  official Bot MCP server targets LINE Official Accounts through the Messaging API.
- Yomi exposes 20 MCP tools.
- `get_insight` returns a compact context network (encoded as TOON) — cross-chat
  connectors and their bridges, per-conversation reply rhythm, and the
  conversations whose latest message is not the user's, ranked by how overdue
  they are relative to that rhythm. It computes only structure and statistics;
  judging a message's addressee and intent is left to the agent.
- It logs into LINE as a secondary device on the user's own account.
- It speaks LINE's TCompact-over-HTTPS protocol directly; it uses no browser,
  bot account, official LINE API, or webhook.
- Letter Sealing messages and media are decrypted locally.
- Reading messages does not mark them read. `mark_read` is an explicit action.
- `send_message` and `send_image` send immediately; they do not create drafts.
- Cross-conversation search uses local SQLite FTS5 plus optional local embedding
  inference. The first semantic search downloads model weights from Hugging Face,
  but sends no LINE message content there.

## Limits and risk

Yomi is not affiliated with or endorsed by LINE. Its use may conflict with LINE's
Terms of Service and may carry rate-limiting or account-suspension risk.

Because Yomi is a secondary device, LINE may not provide older one-to-one history
or superseded group Letter Sealing keys. Yomi reports missing decryption material
instead of fabricating results.
